sections.heading: What is Jetpack Compose?
sections.content: Jetpack Compose is Android’s modern UI toolkit introduced by Google to build native interfaces using Kotlin instead of XML. It follows a declarative approach where developers describe what the UI should look like for a given state. When the state changes, Compose automatically updates the UI. This removes the need for findViewById, XML layouts, and manual UI updates.

sections.heading: Why Use Compose?
items: Automatic UI updates with state changes
Better support for animations and custom UI
Recommended by Google for modern Android apps

sections.heading: Composable Functions
sections.content: Compose UI is built using composable functions. A composable is a Kotlin function annotated with @Composable that describes a piece of UI. Composables can call other composables and combine them to create complex layouts.

sections.heading: State in Compose
sections.content: State drives UI updates in Compose. When a state value changes, Compose automatically recomposes the affected UI. Local state is usually stored using remember { mutableStateOf() }.

sections.heading: Summary
sections.content: Jetpack Compose simplifies Android UI development by replacing XML with Kotlin-based declarative code. Using composables, modifiers, and state-driven updates, developers can build modern, reactive, and maintainable Android interfaces.
